title: Poa linearifolia
taxonomy: Poa linearifolia is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to China, where it is commonly found in mountainous regions. Poa linearifolia is classified as a type of cool-season grass, which means it grows best in the spring or fall when temperatures are cooler. Within the genus Poa, Poa linearifolia is a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Poa linearifolia provides valuable information for botanists and ecologists studying this species in its native habitat.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.
